name: Asymmetric Roles
description: Players occupy different roles with distinct abilities, permissions, information, cameras, or objective responsibilities.
design_notes: Asymmetric roles need more than different ability lists. The design should define role_id assignment, authority ownership, role permissions, camera and UI differences, reconnect restoration, and communication support so a powerful role does not become an exploit or a confusing burden.
design_purpose: Create teamwork by giving players complementary information, tools, or responsibilities.
Support different skill levels and play preferences within the same session.
Make coordination meaningful because no single player has every action or perspective.
player_fantasy: Each player feels uniquely useful and sees why their role matters to the team's success.

parameters.name: role_id
parameters.description: Assigned role identifier used by permissions, UI, abilities, and reconnect restoration.

parameters.name: authority_owner_id
parameters.description: Authority that accepts role-specific actions such as commands, heals, swaps, or station use.

parameters.name: role_permission_policy_id
parameters.description: Permission set that controls role abilities and restricted interactions.

parameters.name: role_ui_profile_id
parameters.description: UI and information layout assigned to the role.

parameters.name: role_assignment_seed
parameters.typical_range: match seed or lobby assignment seed
parameters.description: Deterministic seed used when roles are randomized or balanced during lobby setup.

parameters.name: role_swap_lock_ms
parameters.description: Lockout after assignment or swap that prevents rapid role cycling and griefing.

edge_cases: A player disconnects while holding the only role that can open a required route or revive teammates.
A role swap happens while an ability, camera mode, or restricted interaction is already in progress.
Late joiners need a role that does not invalidate the team's current objective state.
The player with a low-information role cannot understand why the team is failing without better communication tools.
common_bugs: Role-specific UI remains visible after a role change and exposes hidden information to the wrong player.
Server action validation checks player_id but not role_id or role_permission_policy_id.
Reconnect restores the player character but assigns the default role instead of the previous role.
Random role assignment differs between clients because lobby slot ordering is not deterministic.
balancing_notes: Every role needs moments of agency; a support role should not spend most of the session waiting for others.
Powerful roles need responsibility, counterplay, or rotation rules so one player does not dominate the session.
Communication load is part of balance; roles that depend on hidden information need good ping and text tools.
accessibility_notes: Offer roles with lower mechanical intensity and roles with lower communication demands where the game supports them.
Make role responsibilities, available actions, and team dependencies visible in text, not only voice chat.

implementation_notes.unity: Store role_id and permission policy in replicated player state, not only on UI prefabs. Netcode RPCs should include player_id and expected role_id, then the server validates role_permission_policy_id before mutating objectives, vehicles, or abilities.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep role data in an authoritative session dictionary keyed by player_id. Role-specific UI nodes should subscribe to role changes and clear restricted state on swap or disconnect, while RPC handlers reject actions that do not match current role_id.
implementation_notes.unreal: Replicate role_id through PlayerState and gate role abilities through server-side permission checks. Role UI can live in PlayerController, but GameMode or authoritative components should restore roles on seamless travel and reconnect.
implementation_notes.web: Maintain role assignments on the server and send role-specific snapshots that omit hidden information for other roles. Browser UI should rebuild from role_ui_profile_id after reconnect or swap rather than trusting cached panels.

example_games.title: Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes
example_games.note: Uses asymmetric information between the defuser and manual readers.

example_games.title: Deep Rock Galactic
example_games.note: Uses class roles that support different team functions during missions.
